2604.20789.title: Working Memory Constraints Scaffold Learning in Transformers under Data Scarcity
2604.20789.paper_summary: We investigate the integration of human-like working memory constraints into the Transformer architecture and implement several cognitively inspired attention variants, including fixed-width windows based and temporal decay based attention mechanisms. Our modified GPT-2 models are trained from scratch on developmentally plausible datasets (10M and 100M words). Performance is evaluated on grammatical judgment tasks (BLiMP) and alignment with human reading time data. Our results indicate that these cognitively-inspired constraints, particularly fixed-width attention, can significantly improve grammatical accuracy especially when training data is scarce. These constrained models also tend to show a stronger alignment with human processing metrics. The findings suggest that such constraints may serve as a beneficial inductive bias, guiding models towards more robust linguistic representations, especially in data-limited settings.
2604.20789.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究了將類人工作記憶限制整合到Transformer架構中，並實現了幾種受認知啟發的注意力變體，包括基於固定寬度窗口和基於時間衰減的注意力機制。<lb/>我們修改的GPT-2模型是在發展上合理的數據集（1000萬和1億個單詞）上從零開始訓練的。<lb/>性能在語法判斷任務（BLiMP）和與人類閱讀時間數據的對齊上進行評估。<lb/>我們的結果表明，這些受認知啟發的限制，特別是固定寬度注意力，能顯著提高語法準確性，尤其是在訓練數據稀缺的情況下。<lb/>這些受限模型也往往與人類處理指標顯示出更強的對齊。<lb/>這些發現表明，這種限制可能作為有益的歸納偏差，指導模型朝向更穩健的語言表示，特別是在數據有限的情況下。
2604.20789.author: Pranava Madhyastha et.al.
